title: Birla Mandir
tagline: Birla Mandir — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Birla Mandir (Lakshmi Narayan Temple) is a Hindu temple located in Jaipur, India, and is one of many Birla mandirs.
overview.description: Birla Mandir (Lakshmi Narayan Temple) is a Hindu temple located in Jaipur, India, and is one of many Birla mandirs. It was built by the B.M. Birla Foundation in 1988 and is constructed solely of white marble. It is dedicated to the Hindu goddess Lakshmi and god Vishnu (Narayan), whose images appear inside, along with other Hindu gods and goddesses and selections from the Gita and Upanishads. It is located in Jaipur's Tilak Nagar neighborhood, near Moti Dungari hill.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jaipur Development Authority
topPlaces.description: Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) is the agency of the Government of Rajasthan responsible for preparation and implementation of master plan for the Jaipur city in Rajasthan state in India. It is engaged in infrastructural and basic amenity development for Jaipur and also environment conservation and development of rural areas around the city.

topPlaces.name: Moti Dungri
topPlaces.description: Moti Dungri is a Hindu temple complex dedicated to Lord Ganesha in Jaipur, Rajasthan. It was built in 1761 under supervision of Seth Jai Ram Paliwal.

topPlaces.name: Rambagh Palace, Jaipur
topPlaces.description: The Rambagh Palace in Jaipur, Rajasthan is the former residence of the maharaja of Jaipur located 5 miles (8.0 km) outside the walls of the city of Jaipur on Bhawani Singh Road. It was constructed in 1835, and was originally built as a garden retreat for a queen's handmaiden, later serving as a royal guesthouse and hunting lodge.

topPlaces.name: Sawai Mansingh Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Sawai Mansingh Stadium, popularly known as SMS Stadium, is a cricket stadium in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India. It was built in 1969 during the reign of Sawai Man Singh II, the last ruling Maharaja of the state of Jaipur.

topPlaces.name: Sawai Mansingh Indoor Stadium
topPlaces.description: Sawai Mansingh Indoor Stadium is an indoor stadium located in Jaipur, Rajasthan. Facilities include player change rooms and lounge, control and medical rooms, media centre, state-of-the-art-acoustics and parking facilities.

topPlaces.name: Rajasthan Polo Club
topPlaces.description: The Rajasthan Polo Club (formerly Rajputana Polo Club) is a polo club in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India.

topPlaces.name: Central Park, Jaipur
topPlaces.description: Central Park, constructed by the Jaipur Development Authority is the largest park in Jaipur, India. It is located, as its name suggests, in the centre of Jaipur city.

topPlaces.name: Vidhan Bhavan, Jaipur
topPlaces.description: The Vidhan Bhavan is the seat of the Rajasthan Legislative Assembly. It is one of the most modern legislature complexes in India.

items.note: Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) is the agency of the Government of Rajasthan responsible for preparation and implementation of master plan for the Jaipur city in Rajasthan state in India. It is engaged in infrastructural and basic amenity development for Jaipur and also environment conservation and development of rural areas around the city.
Moti Dungri is a Hindu temple complex dedicated to Lord Ganesha in Jaipur, Rajasthan. It was built in 1761 under supervision of Seth Jai Ram Paliwal.

The Rambagh Palace in Jaipur, Rajasthan is the former residence of the maharaja of Jaipur located 5 miles (8.0 km) outside the walls of the city of Jaipur on Bhawani Singh Road. It was constructed in 1835, and was originally built as a garden retreat for a queen's handmaiden, later serving as a royal guesthouse and hunting lodge.

The Sawai Mansingh Stadium, popularly known as SMS Stadium, is a cricket stadium in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India. It was built in 1969 during the reign of Sawai Man Singh II, the last ruling Maharaja of the state of Jaipur.

Sawai Mansingh Indoor Stadium is an indoor stadium located in Jaipur, Rajasthan. Facilities include player change rooms and lounge, control and medical rooms, media centre, state-of-the-art-acoustics and parking facilities.
The Rajasthan Polo Club (formerly Rajputana Polo Club) is a polo club in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India.
Central Park, constructed by the Jaipur Development Authority is the largest park in Jaipur, India. It is located, as its name suggests, in the centre of Jaipur city.
The Vidhan Bhavan is the seat of the Rajasthan Legislative Assembly. It is one of the most modern legislature complexes in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Birla Mandir?
faq.a: The best time to visit Birla Mandir is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Birla Mandir?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (8 km) and the nearest railway station is Jaipur Junction (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Birla Mandir?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Birla Mandir at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Birla Mandir?
faq.a: Stays in Birla Mandir start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Birla Mandir?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jaipur Development Authority, Moti Dungri, Rambagh Palace, Jaipur, Sawai Mansingh Stadium, Sawai Mansingh Indoor Stadium.
